name: interval-occlusion
note: Second fixture of the twin conformance corpus (2026-07-16, occlusion-aware intervalOf). NEUTRAL GROUND: this file belongs to neither twin's directory; both scher/src/society.ts (vitest: test/conformance.interval-occlusion.test.ts) and scher/scher-core/src/lib.rs (cargo: tests/interval_occlusion_fixture.rs) replay these SAME rows and must produce these SAME readings. The scenario mirrors the migrate-shaped edit the app-side placement-laws law 7 fence caught: an event is grounded into an old story's End, that membership edge is then occluded, and the event is grounded into a new story's End instead. Rows are laid verbatim via the one write (lay), including the ~q mode-beats — no layP/lay_p constructor, so both harnesses stay byte-symmetric and guard-free. Expected reads pin intervalOf (current-moment, matching lib.rs's interval_of which reads occlusion as-of now — no asOf parameter on either twin's public signature, so this fixture stays signature-neutral) for both the old and new story's Once/End bounds AFTER the migrate completes, proving the occluded membership edge drops out of the old day's walk and the bare closing edge onto the old End does not resurrect it, while the new day's walk includes it.

rows.content: bare closing onto the old End
rows.subject: ev-migrant

rows.content: the migrate occludes the old membership
rows.subject: mig-act

rows.content: the migrate occludes the old membership [q-occludes]
rows.subject: occ-m-old

rows.content: bare closing onto the new End
rows.subject: ev-migrant
